name: rules
context: Author, lint, unit-test, and inspect Prometheus recording and alerting rules.
info: Prometheus rules turn raw queries into durable operational behavior. Recording rules precompute expensive or commonly reused PromQL, while alerting rules track symptoms over time and emit alert state with labels and annotations. This skill covers authoring rule groups, validating them with `promtool check rules`, unit testing them with `promtool test rules`, and inspecting loaded rules and active alerts from the server.
body:
# Prometheus Rules

Rule files are YAML groups evaluated on a schedule. Recording rules create new series from PromQL expressions, and alerting rules create alert state with labels and annotations that later flow into [[prometheus/alertmanager]].

```yaml
groups:
  - name: api
    interval: 30s
    rules:
      - record: job:http_requests:rate5m
        expr: sum by (job) (rate(http_requests_total[5m]))
      - alert: ApiHighErrorRate
        expr: rate(http_requests_total{code=~"5.."}[5m]) > 0.05
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: page
        annotations:
          summary: API error rate is above 5 percent
```

The official toolchain is explicit:

```bash
promtool check rules rules/*.yml
promtool test rules tests/*.test.yml
curl "$PROMETHEUS_URL/api/v1/rules"
curl "$PROMETHEUS_URL/api/v1/alerts"
```

Unit tests are the key quality bar. They let an agent prove the rule still behaves correctly when raw metrics or label sets change.

sections.title: Recording vs alerting rules
sections.body: Recording rules save the result of a PromQL expression back into the TSDB under a new metric name, which is ideal for expensive or reused aggregations. Alerting rules evaluate a condition and create alert state, optionally requiring it to hold for `for:` before firing. Use recording rules to simplify dashboards and alert expressions; use alerting rules to define operational symptoms.

sections.title: Rule file structure
sections.body: A rule file contains `groups`, and each group contains `rules`. Group-level `interval` controls how often expressions are evaluated. Within an alert, `labels` influence routing and deduplication in [[prometheus/alertmanager]], while `annotations` provide human-readable detail for responders.

sections.title: Testing with promtool
sections.body: The official `promtool test rules` flow feeds synthetic input series into rule files and asserts the expected recording samples or alert states at specific evaluation times. This is how agents prevent regressions when metric names, labels, or thresholds shift. Tests are especially important for `for:` behavior, absent-series logic, and multi-label alert grouping.

links.context: Every rule expression is written in PromQL

PROMETHEUS_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY.description: Skip TLS verification for debugging only
